content.full_text:
May 24, 1990

John Wallace
FPPC
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear John:

Recently I was elected to the State Senate in a special election. I would like to begin paying the debts incurred by my campaign in this election. Please advise me as to whether my understanding of your regulations in this matter is correct.

As far as limits on campaign contributions are concerned, it is my understanding a special election is completely separate from the regular fiscal year. Any money I transferred from my Assembly campaign account to my special election account would be considered a direct contribution to the special election -- and not be counted toward my regular fiscal year limit.

To pay off my campaign debt I must set up a Senate 1992 account. Any campaign contributions I receive now are subject to the regular fiscal year limits. However, I can transfer this money into my special election account to pay off my debt even if the total contribution to the special election exceeds the contributor's fiscal year limits. For example, a person can give $1000 in the special, $1000 in the special run-off and an additional $1000 in the fiscal year and I can transfer the fiscal year $1000 into the special election account to pay off my debt.

The close of the fiscal year is quickly approaching so please advise me as soon as possible whether my understanding of FPPC regulation is correct.

Sincerely,

FRANK HILL
